content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 11 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 208–209 | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_011_page_001.jpg, DEU_011_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 150–151

---

1 And you shall love the LORD your God and you shall keep his charge and his statutes and his ordinances and his commandments all the days.

2 And you shall know today — for [I speak] not to your sons who did not know and who did not see the discipline of the LORD your God — his greatness, his strong hand and his outstretched arm,

3 and his signs and his works which he did in the midst of Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt and to all his land,

4 and what he did to the army of Egypt, to their horses and to their chariots, how he made the waters of the Red Sea [Yam Suph] overflow upon them as they pursued after you, and the LORD destroyed them unto this day,

5 and what he did to you in the wilderness until your coming to this place,

6 and what he did to Dathan and Aviram, sons of Eli'av son of Reuven, how the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their households and their tents and every living thing that was in their wake in the midst of all Yisra'el —

7 but your eyes — they saw every great work of the LORD which he did.

8 And you shall keep every commandment which I am commanding you today, in order that you may be strong and go in and possess the land into which you are crossing to possess it,

9 and in order that you may lengthen your days upon the ground which the LORD swore to your fathers to give to them and to their seed — a land flowing with milk and honey.

10 For the land into which you are going in to possess it — it is not like the land of Egypt from which you came out, where you sowed your seed and watered [it] with your foot like a garden of vegetables.

11 But the land into which you are crossing to possess it — a land of hills and valleys, by the rain of the heavens it drinks water —

12 a land which the LORD your God seeks; the eyes of the LORD your God are always upon it, from the beginning of the year and unto the end of the year.

13 And it shall be, if you indeed hearken to my commandments which I am commanding you today, to love the LORD your God and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul —

14 then I will give the rain of your land in its season, the early rain and the latter rain [yoreh u-malqosh], and you shall gather your grain and your new wine and your fresh oil,

15 and I will give grass in your fields for your livestock, and you shall eat and be satisfied.

16 Be careful lest your heart be deceived and you turn and serve other gods and bow down to them,

17 and the anger of the LORD burn against you and he shut up the heavens and there be no rain, and the ground not yield its produce, and you perish quickly from upon the good land which the LORD is giving you.

18 And you shall put these my words upon your heart and upon your soul, and you shall bind them as a sign upon your hand, and they shall be for frontlets [totafot] between your eyes.

19 And you shall teach them to your sons, speaking of them when you sit in your house and when you walk on the road and when you lie down and when you rise up.

20 And you shall write them on the doorposts [mezuzot] of your house and on your gates,

21 in order that your days and the days of your sons may multiply upon the ground which the LORD swore to your fathers to give them — like the days of the heavens over the earth.

22 For if you carefully keep all this commandment which I am commanding you to do it — to love the LORD your God, to walk in all his ways and to cling to him —

23 then the LORD will drive out all these nations from before you and you shall possess greater and mightier nations than you.

24 Every place where the sole of your foot treads shall be yours — from the wilderness and the Levanon, from the river, the Euphrates River, and unto the western sea [ha-yam ha-acharon] shall be your territory.

25 No man shall stand before you; the LORD your God will put the dread of you and the fear of you upon all the face of the ground that you shall tread, as he spoke to you.

26 See, I am setting before you today a blessing and a curse [berakhah u-qelalah]:

27 the blessing — when you hearken to the commandments of the LORD your God which I am commanding you today;

28 and the curse — if you do not hearken to the commandments of the LORD your God and you turn from the way which I am commanding you today to walk after other gods which you have not known.

29 And it shall be when the LORD your God brings you into the land into which you are going to possess it, that you shall give the blessing upon Mount Gerizim [Har Gerizim] and the curse upon Mount Eival [Har Eival].

30 Are they not across the Yarden, behind the road of the going-down of the sun, in the land of the Canaanite dwelling in the Aravah, opposite Gilgal, beside the oaks [elonei] of Moreh?

31 For you are crossing over the Yarden to go in to possess the land which the LORD your God is giving to you, and you shall possess it and dwell in it.

32 And you shall be careful to do all the statutes and the ordinances which I am setting before you today.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**V.2 — "not to your sons":** The verse is syntactically broken — an anacoluthon in Hebrew. Moshe says "know today — for not your sons..." and then the object of "know" is spelled out in vv.3–7. The awkward syntax is preserved rather than smoothed. The LXX (Vat. p.150) similarly preserves the suspended construction.

**V.4 — Yam Suph:** Hebrew יַם-סוּף — "Sea of Reeds" (traditionally "Red Sea"). Transliterated as Yam Suph per EMET convention since the identification of the body of water is debated.

**V.6 — Dathan and Aviram:** The account refers to the Qorach rebellion of Numbers 16. Notably Qorach himself is not named here — only Dathan and Aviram. The LXX agrees with MT in naming only Dathan and Aviram.

**V.10 — "watered with your foot":** Hebrew וְהִשְׁקִיתָ בְרַגְלְךָ — an unusual idiom. The foot-watering of Egypt likely refers to irrigation systems operated by foot-powered water-lifts (shadoof mechanism). The contrast is between human-engineered irrigation (Egypt) and divine-provision rainfall (Canaan). The LXX renders καὶ κατεπότιζες τῷ ποδί (and you watered with the foot). The idiom is preserved literally.

**V.14 — yoreh u-malqosh:** Hebrew יוֹרֶה וּמַלְקוֹשׁ — "early rain and latter rain." The yoreh (also moreh) is the first rain of autumn (Oct–Nov), the malqosh is the latter rain of spring (Mar–Apr). Together they bracket the agricultural rainy season. The LXX renders πρόιμον καὶ ὄψιμον (early and late [rain]).

**V.18 — totafot:** Hebrew טֹטָפֹת — "frontlets" — the word used for the phylactery-cases worn between the eyes. The term is obscure; LXX renders ἀσάλευτον (immovable thing), which is itself interpretive. "Frontlets" is the established traditional English rendering for this term.

**V.18 — mezuzot:** Hebrew מְזוּזֹת — literally "doorposts." The word refers to the door-frame posts, not to the small container (which is named after the doorpost where it is affixed). Rendered "doorposts" literally.

**V.24 — "ha-yam ha-acharon":** Hebrew הַיָּם הָאַחֲרוֹן — "the latter/western sea" — the Mediterranean, called "latter" (acharon) because Hebrew geographic orientation faces east, making west "behind/after." Rendered "western sea" with the Hebrew in brackets.

**V.26 — berakhah u-qelalah:** Hebrew בְּרָכָה וּקְלָלָה — "blessing and curse." This pairing introduces the covenant alternative that Deuteronomy develops at length (cf. ch. 27–28). The LXX renders εὐλογία καὶ κατάρα (blessing and curse).

**V.29 — Gerizim and Eival:** These twin mountains flanking Shechem (modern Nablus) will be the site of the ceremony described in chs. 27–28. The LXX Vat. p.151 renders Γαριζειν and Γαιβαλ, slight phonetic adaptations. The Hebrew forms are preserved in EMET transliteration.

**V.30 — "oaks of Moreh":** Hebrew אֵלוֹנֵי מֹרֶה — the same sacred site associated with Avraham's first encampment in Canaan (Gen 12:6). The LXX renders ἐπὶ τὴν δρῦν τὴν ὑψηλήν (by the tall oak), a reading that may reflect a different Vorlage or interpretive paraphrase. MT is preserved.

— EMET
